description: Phase 10 Task 1: 650M-INT8 baseline + ESM_PROFILE breakdown + thread-topology sweep + FP32-AMX datapoint on Apple M3 Pro. Sets the bottleneck ranking and the threading default before any Phase 10 change.
hardware: Apple M3 Pro (6 P-cores + 6 E-cores, 12 logical), 18 GB, macOS 26.3.1

profile_breakdown_uniform_int8_pct.note: GEMM (fc1+fc2+qkv+out) ~68%, attention ~27%, glue ~4%. Confirms the P9 GEMM-bound finding. AMX-INT8 (the headline lever) attacks the 68% bucket; PV attention (T6) attacks the 27%.

thread_sweep_uniform_int8_p50_ms.finding: All 12 cores (incl. E-cores) is FASTEST. The P-core-only hypothesis is WRONG on M3 Pro for this workload: grain-4/6 parallel_for over M=2048 creates enough chunks to load-balance across heterogeneous cores, so E-cores add net throughput rather than bottlenecking. The current default (hardware_concurrency=12) is already optimal. T7 = doc fix + recorded negative result; QoS-bias-to-P-cores would likely regress.

fp32_amx_vs_int8_sdot_uniform_p50_ms.finding: FP32+AMX (already wired via ESM_APPLE_AMX=on) is e2e on par with INT8 SDOT (within 1%), confirming the spike at the model level. So FP32+AMX is a viable quality-preserving Mac path but NOT faster. A material speedup requires AMX-INT8 (BNNSGraph) — projected ~2x on the 68% GEMM bucket -> ~1.5x e2e.

baseline_to_beat_p9.note: P9 varlen/HF baselines reused (no code change yet; HF varlen is ~150s/iter and unchanged). This run measured uniform INT8 at 2826ms p50 (slightly faster than P9's 2962, within run-to-run variance).
